Tests the behavior of the Function constructor with duplicate parameter names.

# Purpose
This code is a short script that tests the behavior of the JavaScript `Function` constructor when given duplicate parameter names. It attempts to create a new function with two parameters named `a` and returns the value of `a`. If the creation of the function fails and throws an error, the `catch` block executes, which contains an `assert(false)` statement. This indicates that the script expects the function creation to succeed without errors, and if it does not, the assertion will fail, signaling an unexpected behavior. The script uses strict mode, which enforces stricter parsing and error handling in JavaScript.
